auto_posts.title: Azure Local Update — February 5, 2026
auto_posts.subtitle: Documentation changes
auto_posts.content:
## Azure Local Documentation Updates: Key Changes for Operations Teams

Microsoft updated Azure Local deployment documentation with critical terminology changes. The platform previously referenced "Azure Local" now consistently uses "Azure Local instance" when describing specific deployments. For example, VM prerequisites now require "Access to one or more VM images on your Azure Local instance" instead of "on your Azure Local."

**Impact**: Scripts and automation referencing old terminology may need updates. The SQL Server deployment guide restructured content—moving from inline description to bulleted list format for workload types like "Online transaction processing (OLTP) workloads."

**Action Required**: - Update deployment scripts to match new "Azure Local instance" terminology - Review Azure CLI commands that now explicitly state "enter the following command" instead of just "Type:" - Verify ARM templates align with updated resource naming conventions

These changes affect deployment accuracy and consistency across Azure Local environments.

Updated documentation: aks-create-clusters-portal • sql-server-23h2 • hci-vm-prerequisites • hci-vm-sign-in-set-subscription • create-arc-virtual-machines • whats-new
